behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: The real failure is at a module boundary: evaluate-stage logic plus artifact persistence keying. A unit test of key-selection logic alone can miss regressions in how the command is wired to the artifact writer.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage end-to-end with a real local artifact store (filesystem or test DB). Assert a `grounding` artifact is created/updated, `plan` is unchanged by this step, and persisted metadata/type match expected grounding semantics.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on read/write interaction across implement flow and artifact store. A pure unit test can validate branching but may miss incorrect artifact lookup keys or write-target wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Seed artifact storage with a `grounding` artifact, execute implement-stage flow, and verify: (1) grounding is consumed (or referenced in context build), (2) output is persisted to `plan`, and (3) `grounding` remains intact after the run.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: The failure appears when deep-dive decision logic interacts with artifact-writing side effects. Verifying only the conditional function is insufficient if command wiring still performs writes.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content that already contains diagnosis, run deep-dive step with real local artifact persistence, and assert no write/update occurs to `plan` while expected deep-dive outputs (if any) are still produced.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a cross-step pipeline guarantee. The defect class is state propagation across sequential planning-chain stages, which is a workflow-level boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full chain in sequence within the real pipeline harness using deterministic fixtures for step outputs where possible. Assert grounding produced in evaluate-stage is present/used in implement-stage context and that final `plan` content reflects that grounding-derived constraints or facts.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: This is fallback behavior across artifact-read miss handling plus write safety. Real failure is in interaction between absence handling and persistence side effects, not just local branching logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement-stage with no `grounding` artifact but with other unrelated artifacts pre-seeded. Verify successful completion, valid `plan` emission, no mutation/corruption of unrelated artifacts, and stable artifact index/state after run.
